rules.title: Export clean, analysis-ready data before connecting to Tableau
rules.body: Tableau performs best when connected to a flat, pre-aggregated dataset rather than raw claims tables. Do any filtering, joining, and variable construction in SAS first.
list: Export from SAS as a .csv or .xlsx — avoid live ODBC connections to raw claims tables
Include only the columns and rows the visualization needs
Name columns clearly — Tableau will use them as field labels directly
Date fields should be formatted as actual SAS date values (not stored as numbers)

rules.title: Design dashboards for the audience, not the analyst
rules.body: Dashboards shared with leadership or external partners need to be self-explanatory. Avoid jargon and keep the layout focused.
list: Limit each dashboard to one primary question it answers
Use a title and subtitle that describe the data source, time period, and population
Include a data-last-updated date on every published view
Avoid more than 3–4 charts per dashboard tab

rules.title: Use ACHI brand colors and maintain accessibility
rules.body: Consistent color use across ACHI outputs reinforces brand identity and ensures charts are readable by team members with color vision differences.
list: Primary: ACHI Navy #00558c, Teal #16a0b3, Maroon #762146
Use diverging palettes (blue-to-red) for comparisons, sequential (light-to-dark) for magnitude
Never rely on color alone to convey meaning — use labels or patterns as backup
Test dashboards with a color-blindness simulator before sharing externally

rules.title: Keep published workbooks performant
rules.body: Large or slow Tableau workbooks frustrate users and may time out on Tableau Server.
list: Use extracts instead of live connections for large datasets
Filter to only the years and populations needed before publishing
Avoid row-level calculations that could be pre-computed in SAS
Use the Performance Recorder (Help → Record Performance) to identify slow views

rules.title: Save final workbooks to the G:\ and publish to Tableau Server
rules.body: All published Tableau workbooks should follow the same naming and organization standards as other final outputs.
list: Save the .twbx (packaged workbook) to G:\DATA\AAA Analytic Projects\[Project]\Final Output
Publish to the appropriate project folder on Tableau Server
Set permissions so only the ACHI team can edit; stakeholders get viewer access
Version the file name the same way as SAS programs: append v0.x YYYYMMDD
